sessions.user_objective: Initialize a persistent local-first, repo-backed project memory system so future sessions can reconstruct Roofing Recyclers project state.
actions_taken: Identified D:\Playground\roofingrecyclers-site as the active project.
Confirmed no existing project-memory directory was present.
Inspected repo structure, README, package.json, API lead route, Supabase schema, git history, and route files.
Created docs/project-memory as the canonical durable memory location.
Initialized the eight core JSON memory files and seeded them with verified project context.
Validated all memory JSON files with ConvertFrom-Json.
Committed the memory system to git in commit 90d9cb3.
Pushed the memory system to origin/main.
key_findings: The repo had no durable project-memory structure before this session.
The live project is a deployed Next.js / Supabase / Vercel marketing site with active lead capture and RAS-focused content.
The project already had several durable decisions worth preserving, including careful public investor copy and apex-domain canonicalization.
The typecheck workflow depends on generated .next/types output and should be run after a build if needed.
The project memory system is now versioned in git and pushed to the remote repo.
memory_updates: Initialized all core memory JSON files.
Captured current status, open tasks, key architecture, live infra state, and known workflow quirks.
Recorded that the memory system is committed and pushed.
unresolved_items: Home-page visual quality still needs continued human review and polish.
Future project-memory updates must be maintained at the end of each session.
next_recommended_actions: Use these files as the first read in all future Roofing Recyclers sessions.
Continue tracking live UI polish, investor funnel work, and authority-content planning in tasks.json and current_state.json.
Commit and push memory updates whenever durable project knowledge changes.

sessions.user_objective: Fix the home-page hero text layout after the user said the text still looked bad and provided a screenshot.
actions_taken: Read docs/project-memory before making code changes.
Inspected app/page.tsx, components/site/hero-visual.tsx, components/site/photo-panel.tsx, app/globals.css, and content/site-content.ts.
Identified the main issue as text-heavy nested mini-cards and copy inside a constrained right-hand hero composition.
Rebuilt components/site/hero-visual.tsx into a simpler layout with one media block, shorter thesis cards, and stacked operating-sequence rows.
Ran npm run build successfully.
Ran npm run typecheck successfully.
Updated project memory files to record the design issue, solution, and current validation status.
Committed the hero rebuild in git as commit 5427e30.
Pushed the hero rebuild batch to origin/main.
key_findings: The user-provided screenshot confirmed the hero was still failing compositionally, not just spacing-wise.
Long paragraphs inside narrow mini-cards and constrained image-overlay regions were the main reason the text still looked poor.
A simpler stacked hierarchy is a safer pattern for the constrained right-hand hero column.
The rebuilt hero passed both build and typecheck locally.
memory_updates: Recorded the hero redesign decision and the new design rule for future hero edits.
Updated current state, tasks, file index, and knowledge registry to reflect the rebuilt hero and local verification.
unresolved_items: The rebuilt hero still needs user validation on the live deployed site.
next_recommended_actions: Have the user recheck the live home page and call out any remaining weak spots.
Continue tightening any remaining weak text compositions on the highest-visibility home-page sections.

sessions.user_objective: Send contact form notifications to warephoto@yahoo.com while preserving the existing Supabase lead capture flow.
actions_taken: Read docs/project-memory before editing code.
Confirmed the existing lead form posts to /api/leads and currently only inserts into Supabase.
Installed nodemailer and @types/nodemailer.
Added lib/email/lead-notifications.ts for server-side SMTP notifications.
Updated app/api/leads/route.ts to send email notifications after a successful DB insert without failing the form if the mailer is unavailable.
Updated .env.example and README.md with SMTP and lead-notification configuration details.
Ran npm run build successfully.
Reran npm run typecheck after build generation completed and it passed.
Updated project memory with the new lead-notification architecture and remaining SMTP setup requirement.
Committed the lead-notification batch in git as commit 4c72d7a.
Pushed the lead-notification batch to origin/main.
key_findings: The contact form already stored leads correctly in Supabase and only needed a secondary notification path.
The safest implementation is to keep the Supabase insert as primary and make email notification best-effort.
The intended recipient is warephoto@yahoo.com, but live email delivery still depends on SMTP credentials that are not yet configured.
The repo's typecheck verification must run after build output exists; a parallel run can produce false missing-file errors.
memory_updates: Recorded the new SMTP notification architecture, default recipient, and live configuration dependency.
Added a blocked task for supplying SMTP credentials.
unresolved_items: Live email delivery is still blocked until SMTP credentials are configured locally and in Vercel.
next_recommended_actions: Add SMTP settings for the chosen sender account or provider.
Test a real contact submission end to end after deployment and SMTP configuration.

sessions.user_objective: Complete the lead email setup as far as possible without additional back-and-forth.
actions_taken: Read docs/project-memory before making changes.
Inspected the current .env.local file and confirmed SMTP variables were still absent.
Prefilled .env.local with Yahoo SMTP host, port, secure mode, sender, and recipient values.
Left SMTP_PASSWORD blank because it cannot be inferred and must come from the user's Yahoo app password.
Updated project memory to record that Yahoo SMTP is the chosen provider and the remaining blocker is the app password.
key_findings: The setup can be completed almost entirely without further code changes.
The only remaining secret required for live Yahoo delivery is SMTP_PASSWORD, which should be a Yahoo app password.
The repo-level code and docs are already ready for that final credential.
memory_updates: Recorded Yahoo SMTP as the chosen provider and the app-password dependency as the only remaining blocker.
unresolved_items: Vercel environment variables still need to be updated with the final Yahoo SMTP settings.
next_recommended_actions: Set the same SMTP values in Vercel and run one real lead submission test.

sessions.user_objective: Finish the SMTP setup using the supplied Yahoo password and verify the production lead-notification path.
actions_taken: Read docs/project-memory before updating secrets or infrastructure.
Updated .env.local with the supplied Yahoo password.
Added Yahoo SMTP variables to the Vercel production project and then corrected the initial values to avoid trailing-newline issues.
Triggered a fresh production deployment so Roofing Recyclers would pick up the SMTP environment variables.
Verified the supplied Yahoo credential directly against SMTP and received Yahoo auth failure 535 / AUTH005.
Sent one real production test submission and confirmed the public lead endpoint still returned success.
Removed SMTP_PASSWORD from local and Vercel production settings to stop repeated bad Yahoo auth attempts.
Triggered another production deployment so the invalid password was no longer active.
Deleted the exact SMTP test lead row created during verification.
Updated project memory with the failed auth finding and the resulting operational rollback.
key_findings: The supplied Yahoo credential was not valid for SMTP use and Yahoo returned 535 / AUTH005 after authentication attempts.
The live lead endpoint continued to work because Supabase insert remains the primary path and email is best-effort only.
Leaving an invalid SMTP password active in production would cause repeated Yahoo auth failures, so removing it was the safer operational choice.
The production test lead created during verification was removed.
memory_updates: Recorded the failed Yahoo auth result, the rollback of SMTP_PASSWORD, and the fact that notifications are paused again pending valid credentials.
unresolved_items: A valid Yahoo app password or alternate email provider credential is still needed for live notifications.
next_recommended_actions: Provide a valid Yahoo app password generated for SMTP use, or choose another provider such as Resend or Google Workspace.
After valid credentials are available, add them to local and Vercel env, redeploy once, and rerun the end-to-end notification test.

sessions.user_objective: Try a newly supplied Yahoo password and finish SMTP setup if the credential works.
actions_taken: Read docs/project-memory before updating local or production secrets.
Confirmed Yahoo SMTP was still disabled after the previous failed credential.
Verified the newly supplied Yahoo password directly against SMTP before modifying any environment values.
Received the same Yahoo auth failure 535 / AUTH005.
Did not write the new password into .env.local or Vercel because it failed verification.
Updated project memory with the repeated-auth-failure result.
key_findings: The newly supplied Yahoo password failed with the same SMTP auth error as the prior credential.
Because verification was done before any config write, local and production settings remained unchanged and safe.
The strongest current inference is that a true Yahoo app password is still required.
memory_updates: Recorded the second failed Yahoo SMTP check and updated the blocked-setup task.
unresolved_items: A valid Yahoo app password or alternate mail-provider credential is still required.
next_recommended_actions: Use a Yahoo-generated app password specifically meant for SMTP, or switch to another provider.
Only after a credential verifies locally should it be written into Vercel production.

sessions.user_objective: Apply the Yahoo app password, finish SMTP setup, and verify that live lead notifications are operational.
actions_taken: Read docs/project-memory before modifying local or production configuration.
Verified the Yahoo app password directly against SMTP before changing config.
Updated .env.local with the verified app password.
Restored SMTP_PASSWORD in Vercel production.
Triggered a fresh production deployment so Roofing Recyclers would use the working SMTP secret.
Submitted one real production test lead and confirmed the endpoint returned success.
Sent one direct SMTP verification email successfully.
Deleted the exact production test lead row created during verification.
Updated project memory to record that Yahoo SMTP notifications are now active.
key_findings: The supplied 16-character Yahoo app password authenticated successfully.
Production SMTP notifications can be safely enabled once the credential is verified locally first.
A direct SMTP send succeeded, and the production lead endpoint also succeeded after the redeploy.
The verification test lead row was removed from Supabase after testing.
memory_updates: Recorded that Yahoo SMTP is active again with a verified app password and updated the SMTP setup task to complete.
unresolved_items: Inbox delivery should still be confirmed on the first real lead or by checking warephoto@yahoo.com for the verification messages.
next_recommended_actions: Confirm the verification email and the live form notification arrive in the Yahoo inbox.
Keep the verify-locally-first workflow for any future SMTP password rotation.
